description: Human referent clarity ratings for RQ1 (1=ambiguous, 5=explicit entity anchors). Wave 6b: unstructured plain baseline scores hydrated from Cursor-proxy offline adjudication (data/eval/rq1_plain_llm_live_proxy_wave6b.json); ADL rows scored via prompts/judge_referent_clarity.md.

study_protocol.purpose: Human inter-rater referent-clarity study to supplement LLM-as-judge RQ1 pilots (AAMAS reviewer feedback)
study_protocol.scale: 1-5 integers; higher = clearer entity/referent anchors in L2 prose

rating_fields.referent_clarity: Primary human score on ADL L2 (required for inclusion in human summary)
rating_fields.referent_clarity_b: Second rater score on ADL L2 (same rubric; enables ICC placeholder)
rating_fields.referent_clarity_fair_plain: Paired fair-plain L2 score (same wording, structure stripped via adl_to_fair_plain)
rating_fields.referent_clarity_plain_llm: Unstructured plain-LLM score (slug-matched baseline prose from plain_discovery_*.md)
rating_fields.rating_completed_at: ISO-8601 timestamp when primary rating submitted
rating_fields.notes: Free-text flags (e.g. adjudication needed when |referent_clarity - referent_clarity_b| >= 2)

instructions: Score L2 prose only; ignore L1 YAML and L3 fenced blocks when rating the ADL arm
Use the same rubric as LLM judges but do not copy proxy scores into referent_clarity
Flag entries where |rater - rater_b| >= 2 for third-party adjudication
Fair-plain arm: rate stripped L2 from adl_to_fair_plain on the same discovery_path
study_protocol.llm_proxy_reference: docs/experiments/rq1_llm_judge_summary.json (reference only; not a substitute for human referent_clarity)

entries.scenario: Peripheral Attention Trap — graph peripheral node concentration while value consolidates toward a hidden sink; align with aml-attention-trap monitoring signals
entries.notes: Cursor proxy judges only (no user API keys); Claude unavailable in region

llm_judge_openai.rationale: Entity anchors are mostly explicit (nodes, actors, sink accounts), but one or two abstract phrases still require light inference about exact operational ownership.

llm_judge_composer.rationale: Referents remain traceable sentence-to-sentence with clear naming of systems, nodes, and beneficiary accounts, and no unresolved pronoun chains.

llm_judge_openai_plain.rationale: The fair-plain rendering preserves explicit node and account references, with only minor abstraction around ownership boundaries.

llm_judge_composer_plain.rationale: Referent flow stays explicit in plain L2, and each mechanism maps to a named entity without ambiguity.

llm_judge_openai_plain_llm.rationale: Demonstratives and bare pronouns (they/it/this/that) dominate; only sparse generic anchors remain, forcing repeated inference.

llm_judge_composer_plain_llm.rationale: Heading states the AML pattern, yet every sentence leans on pronouns without pinning which accounts, corridors, or graph roles are referenced.

entries.scenario: Smurfing Pattern — sub-threshold deposit structuring across a beneficial-owner network; align with aml-smurfing CTR threshold heuristics
entries.notes: Cursor proxy judges only (no user API keys); Claude unavailable in region

llm_judge_openai.rationale: Entities and phases are concretely named throughout (sub-threshold deposits, linked accounts, consolidation transfer), leaving no meaningful referent ambiguity.

llm_judge_composer.rationale: Referents are consistently identifiable, with only minor underspecification in a few broad category phrases.

llm_judge_openai_plain.rationale: Plain L2 still keeps each actor, threshold, and transfer phase explicitly grounded by name.

llm_judge_composer_plain.rationale: Most referents are explicit and traceable, though a small number of generalized category mentions reduce maximal clarity.

llm_judge_openai_plain_llm.rationale: CTR thresholds and structuring vocabulary ground the AML story; pronouns still blur which depositors versus consolidation accounts execute each hop.

llm_judge_composer_plain_llm.rationale: Regulatory jargon provides partial anchors, though they/it/them swaps keep counterparties ambiguous from sentence to sentence.

entries.scenario: Crypto Mixer Exposure — wallet activity linked to tumbler contracts with peel-chain off-ramp patterns; align with aml-crypto-mix monitoring signals
entries.notes: MiMo batch 2026-05-23; L2 rephrase for validator that+have false positive. Cursor proxy judges only (no user API keys); Claude unavailable in region

llm_judge_openai.rationale: Core entities are named clearly (wallets, mixer contracts, peel-chain, off-ramp), with minor abstraction in comparative statements.

llm_judge_composer.rationale: Referent tracing is reliable across paragraphs, but a few high-level contrasts still depend on implied pairing rather than explicit restatement.

llm_judge_openai_plain.rationale: Plain L2 retains explicit naming for wallet behavior and contract interactions, with only small inferential gaps in comparative phrasing.

llm_judge_composer_plain.rationale: The prose keeps referents mostly explicit and stable across steps, with minor underspecification in shorthand contrasts.

llm_judge_openai_plain_llm.rationale: Mixer hops, peels, fiat spikes, and wallet clusters mostly name observable entities yet chained they/it tails still muddy upstream custody.

llm_judge_composer_plain_llm.rationale: Despite pronoun-heavy phrasing, each paragraph restates wallets, hops, fiat ramps, or peel chains so laundering roles largely stay traceable.
